skill.name: crawl4ai
skill.description: Use Crawl4AI to crawl web pages, extract structured content, capture screenshots, and export pages as PDFs.
skill.summary: Crawl, extract, screenshot, and archive web pages with Crawl4AI.
skill.author: CK991357

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and inline backticks, not executable Ruby or shell code. The real risk is network crawling, especially documented anti-detection behavior and batch or deep crawling features. No prompt injection, malware intent, weak cryptographic implementation, or obfuscated payload was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Anti-Detection Crawling Features
medium_findings.description: The skill documents browser fingerprint rotation, incognito mode, disabled automation controls, and disabled web security settings. These features can support legitimate testing, but they can also help bypass site anti-bot controls if misused.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly describe anti-crawl and browser fingerprint evasion behavior. The finding is medium rather than high because the file is documentation for a crawler, not confirmed malicious code.

medium_findings.title: Broad External Web Crawling and Capture
medium_findings.description: The skill enables single page crawling, deep crawling, batch URL processing, screenshots, and PDF exports. This creates privacy, authorization, and terms-of-service risk when used against sites without permission.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly shows network crawling and capture capabilities. Legitimate research use is possible, so the concern is controlled-use risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick executions, but the file is Markdown. The cited backticks are fenced JSON examples and inline parameter names, with no executable Ruby or shell script present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows Markdown instructions and JSON request examples. I found no command execution primitive or shell invocation in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Entropy Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hit at the front matter description and the high entropy warning at file start appear caused by metadata, Chinese text, emoji, and dense examples. No cryptographic implementation or hidden payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain YAML front matter, not cryptographic code. The file is readable Markdown, so the entropy signal does not indicate obfuscation here.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Anti-Detection Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill describes rotating browser identity signals and disabling browser protections. Marketplace users should restrict this to authorized crawling and compliance testing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wording directly names anti-detection browser behavior. It is a documented capability rather than inferred intent.

content.value_statement: Web research often needs clean page text, screenshots, PDFs, and structured fields from many URLs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Crawl4AI modes with safe parameter structure.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required nested call structure for Crawl4AI modes.
Guides single page scraping with markdown, HTML, or text output.
Supports deep crawling with depth, page count, strategy, and keyword options.
Describes batch crawling for multiple URLs with concurrency limits.
Documents CSS-based structured extraction using selectors and field definitions.
Covers screenshot capture and PDF export with base64 outputs.
limitations: The skill is documentation only and does not include executable crawler code.
LLM extraction mode is described as not fully deployed.
Reliable extraction requires accurate CSS selectors for target pages.
Users must handle website permission, robots.txt, and terms of service.

use_cases.title: Research Source Collection
use_cases.description: Collect readable page content, links, and images from approved sources for analysis.

use_cases.title: Product Page Monitoring
use_cases.description: Extract product names, prices, and details from owned or authorized catalog pages.

use_cases.title: Compliance Evidence Capture
use_cases.description: Save screenshots and PDFs of web pages for audits, reviews, or records.

prompt_templates.title: Scrape One Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Crawl4AI to scrape this approved URL as markdown. Include links and images, and keep the parameters nested correctly: [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Capture Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Crawl4AI to capture a screenshot and PDF for this approved page. Return a concise summary of what was captured: [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Extract Structured Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Crawl4AI extract mode for this page. Extract these fields with CSS selectors: [fields and selectors]. URL: [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Deep Crawl
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Crawl4AI deep crawl for this authorized site. Use max depth, page limits, keywords, and URL filters: [site and goals].

output_examples.input: A request to scrape one approved documentation page.
output_examples.output: A concise page summary, main headings, extracted links, image references, and notes about missing content.

output_examples.input: A request to capture a competitor landing page for an authorized review.
output_examples.output: A capture report confirming screenshot and PDF creation, with page title, final URL, and visible page sections.

output_examples.input: A request to extract article title, author, date, and body text using selectors.
output_examples.output: A structured content summary with the requested fields and warnings for selectors that returned no match.

best_practices: Start with one approved URL before scaling to batch or deep crawling.
Use precise CSS selectors and test them on small samples first.
Respect robots.txt, site terms, rate limits, and data privacy rules.
anti_patterns: Do not crawl sites where you lack authorization or a lawful basis.
Do not use anti-detection settings to bypass access controls.
Do not request broad deep crawls without page limits and URL filters.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents use Crawl4AI for scraping, crawling, extraction, screenshots, and PDF export.

faq.question: Does the skill include crawler source code?
faq.answer: No. It is a usage guide for a Crawl4AI tool interface.

faq.question: Which output formats are documented?
faq.answer: The guide describes markdown, HTML, text, screenshots, PDFs, and structured extracted fields.

faq.question: Can it extract data without CSS selectors?
faq.answer: The guide says CSS extraction is the practical path. LLM extraction is not fully deployed.

faq.question: Is batch crawling supported?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide documents batch URL processing with concurrency and page limits.

faq.question: What safety checks should users apply?
faq.answer: Use only authorized targets, limit crawl size, respect site rules, and avoid bypassing access controls.
